feedback.text: In recent years, we have witnessed a growing and alarming trend: the shutdown of online games, digital services, and servers, often rendering purchased games unplayable and erasing years of community engagement, creativity, and cultural value. This needs to stop. We advocate for legal and technical frameworks that ensure online and digital-only games can remain accessible even after official support ends. Gamers should retain access to the products they purchase. If a game is sold with online features, companies must be transparent about the game's lifecycle and provide options when services end. We demand clearer communication from game publishers about server shutdowns, DRM restrictions, and the long-term accessibility of digital content. Thank you.
